description: Samarasena Arachchige SumedaVs. Urban Development Authority Samarasena Arachchige SumedaVs. Urban Development Authority View More before Hon. K. M. S. Dissanayake, J
parties: Samarasena Arachchige SumedaVs. Urban Development Authority Samarasena Arachchige SumedaVs. Urban Development Authority View More
judgement_by: Hon. K. M. S. Dissanayake, J
keywords: Appeal from a judgment of the High Court established by Article 154P(1) of the Constitution as amended made in the exercise of revisionary jurisdiction vested in it by Article 154P(3)(b) the Constitution as amended-application made to a Magistrate Court under and in terms of section 28A(3) of the Urban Development Authority Act No. 41 of 1978 as amended for a demolition order-in an application under 28A(3) of the Urban Development Authority Act the pertinent question for decision by Court is whether construction in question has been erected by the party noticed by virtue of a valid permit-What are the defence available to a party noticed in an application under the section 28A(3) of the Urban Development Authority Act-On whom does the burden of proof lie.
legistation: Article 154P(6) of the Constitution-Sections 8J(1) and 8K(1), 28A(3) of the Urban Development Authority Act No. 41 of 1978 as amended-Sections 3, 101 and 102 of the Evidence Ordinance.
